body_txt: There often are times when people eat. For most a few times a day. There are other times when people absolutely stuff themselves . This hopefully doesn't happen as often. But when animated characters do it, it goes right to their stomachs and increases their size and figure in a similar manner to filling a balloon. This new-found girth usually goes away pretty easily, but can stay around for a while. A usually-minor form of this, known in slang occasionally as the "food baby", is Truth in Television . Usually used for comedy, but is also a somewhat prominent Fetish that pops up in fanfics and fanart. Also, there are times when people don't have anything (and especially a lack of protein) to eat for a long time , and then they play the trope exceptionally straight. It's called kwashiorkor . Ascites , a condition where the abdomen swells up due to a build-up of fluid, is a symptom of several serious conditions, including liver failure, heart failure or even cancer. Anyone displaying this trope in real life would therefore likely be in need of medical attention. This trope is especially popular in Zany Cartoons to the point where attempting to list every example would probably end up with its subpage being longer than all the other subpages together. A Gigantic Gulp drink is often a cause of Balloon Belly. May lead to Temporary Bulk Change . Its logical conclusion is "Pop!" Goes the Human . Compare Inflating Body Gag , which is when a character's entire body inflates like a balloon. Also compare Dinner Deformation , Trampoline Tummy , Traveling-Pipe Bulge , and Weight Taller . Contrast Black-Hole Belly . No Real Life Examples, Please! noreallife
